_note: OpenCode resolves custom commands from markdown files in .opencode/commands/ (project) or ~/.config/opencode/commands/ (global). The preferred install is to copy integrations/opencode/commands/*.md into one of those locations — that's what gives you per-verb autocompletion. The JSON form below is an equivalent alternative for users who prefer to keep their commands in opencode.jsonc.

lib-session-start.template: Start a new Librarian session via the start_session MCP tool. Arguments ($ARGUMENTS): optional title; the token --private sets visibility: agent_private. Defaults: harness: opencode, cwd: <current>, project_key: <project root>, source_ref: opencode:project:<cwd>:session:${OPENCODE_SESSION_ID} (fallback opencode:project:<cwd>), visibility: common unless --private, capture_mode: summary. Sensitivity check first: if identity/secrets/personal/sensitive-debugging signals are present and --private was not supplied, confirm before starting a common session. After calling, report the session_id, visibility, and a one-paragraph baseline; keep the session_id in conversational state.

lib-session-list.template: List resumable Librarian sessions via list_sessions. Scope project_key and cwd to the current project; only set harness: opencode if the user clearly wants OpenCode-only results. Include archived/deleted only when $ARGUMENTS contains --archived/--deleted. Render numbered entries with status, title, project, harness, source, last activity, and the first next step. Remind that numbers are agent-side scratch; tool calls take the canonical session_id. Never auto-resume.

lib-session-resume.description: Resume a Librarian session (fetch handover + attach)
lib-session-resume.template: Resume a Librarian session via continue_session. Arguments ($ARGUMENTS): <number|session_id>. Numbers resolve from the last /lib-session-list response; if no list was run and the argument is not a ses_ id, ask the user to run /lib-session-list first. Defaults: attach: true (the default), target_harness: opencode, target_cwd: <current>, target_source_ref: opencode:project:<cwd>:session:${OPENCODE_SESSION_ID} (fallback opencode:project:<cwd>), format: opencode. Display the handover text returned by the tool; keep the resumed session_id in state.

lib-session-checkpoint.description: Checkpoint the active Librarian session (updates rolling_summary, stays active)
lib-session-checkpoint.template: Checkpoint the active Librarian session via checkpoint_session. Use the session_id you've been carrying since the last start/resume; ask the user if you don't have one. Build the call: summary (work since session start or previous checkpoint), decisions, files_touched, commands_run, open_questions, next_steps. Confirm the new rolling_summary in one sentence.

lib-session-pause.template: Pause the active Librarian session via pause_session. Use the carried session_id (or ask). Build summary + next_steps. Activity on the session implicitly resumes it later.

lib-session-end.template: End the active Librarian session via end_session. Use the carried session_id. Build summary + decisions + files_touched + commands_run + open_questions + next_steps + optional candidate_memories. Surface candidate durable memories as a numbered list but DO NOT auto-promote — wait for explicit user direction to call promote_session_fact, remember, or propose_memory. ended is terminal; to continue, start a new session with metadata.continues_from referencing this id.

lib-session-archive.description: Archive a Librarian session (hide from default lists)
lib-session-archive.template: Archive a Librarian session via archive_session. Arguments ($ARGUMENTS): <number|session_id> [reason...]. Resolve numbers from the last list. Confirm the session is archived; hidden from default list, surfaced with --archived or include_archived. Restorable.

lib-session-restore.description: Restore an archived or soft-deleted Librarian session
lib-session-restore.template: Restore a session via restore_session. Arguments ($ARGUMENTS): <number|session_id>. Owner-or-admin only — surface ownership errors verbatim. Report the restored session's new status (its prior_status, falling back to paused).

lib-session-delete.template: Soft-delete a session via delete_session. Arguments ($ARGUMENTS): <number|session_id> [reason...]. Confirm with the user before sending (soft-delete is reversible via restore but removes from default lists). Owner-or-admin only — surface ownership errors verbatim and stop.

lib-session-search.description: Search Librarian sessions by event content
lib-session-search.template: Search session summaries and events via search_sessions. Arguments ($ARGUMENTS): the query. Scope project_key to the current project unless cross-project search is wanted. Include archived/deleted only when $ARGUMENTS contains --archived/--deleted (deleted requires admin). Limit 5 by default. Render numbered matches with title, status, project, id.

lib-session-status.description: Show the currently attached Librarian session for this harness/source
lib-session-status.template: Show the currently attached Librarian session. Resolution: (1) use carried session_id if any; (2) else list_sessions scoped to harness: opencode and current cwd, take the most recent by last_activity_at; (3) else report no session attached. For the chosen session, call get_session and render title/status/visibility/project/created-vs-current harness+source/summaries/next_steps/last activity, then list the last 5 events via list_session_events.
